pr.title: match most of ftNn_Init. still needs splitting and some inline/floatmath work.
pr.author: BR-

summary: Matched most of Nana's ftNn_Init.c by replacing many placeholder stubs with concrete Ice Climbers special-move and partner-sync logic, updating Popo/Nana shared types, and correcting a data split boundary. The decomp.dev report credited 24 new full function matches and 4 partial improvements, raising matched code by 3168 bytes. The PR title explicitly notes remaining work around splitting, inline behavior, and float/math matching, so the result was substantial but not a complete cleanup of the unit.
what_changed: Implemented a large block of previously undecompiled ftNn_Init functions, including ftNn_Init_8012300C, ftNn_Init_8012309C, ftNn_Init_801230D0, fn_80123218, ftNn_Init_801232A4, ftNn_Init_801233F8, ftNn_Init_80123720, ftNn_Init_8012378C, ftNn_Init_801237F8, ftNn_Init_801238E4, ftNn_Init_80123954, ftNn_Init_80123B10, ftNn_Init_80123B3C, and ftNn_Init_80123BF0.
Filled in many Nana/Popo special move callbacks for SpecialHi and SpecialS, replacing empty functions or placeholder /// markers with animation, physics, and collision logic.
Changed ftNn_Init public signatures from HSD_GObj* to Fighter_GObj* in the implemented code, aligning call sites with fighter-specific access through GET_FIGHTER.

Added dependencies needed by the new logic, including efLib pause/resume callbacks, common fall/fall-special APIs, ft_081B, ft_0892, ftanim, ftcommon, ft/inlines, lb_00B0, player lookup, and MSL math for sinf/cosf.
Replaced a Vec3 array representation of collision data with ftCollisionBox instances: ftNn_Unk_28 in ftNn_Init.c and ftNn_Unk2_803CDD60 in ftNn_Unk0.c.

Updated ftPopo/types.h so ftPopo_FighterVars.x2240 is a Vec rather than three u32 words, expanded ftIceClimberAttributes with named float fields such as xB0, xB4, xD0, x12C, x130, x134, x138, x13C, x140, x144, x148, and x14C, and added a motion-vars struct used by ftNn_Init_80123954.
Adjusted config/GALE01/splits.txt for ftNn_Init.c data end from 0x803CDD60 to 0x803CDD5C, reflecting the data-layout change.

The automated report showed 24 newly fully matched functions and 4 unmatched functions improved: ftPp_SpecialS_0_Coll to 99.13%, ftPp_SpecialS_1_Coll to 92.15%, ftNn_Init_80123954 to 74.59%, and ftNn_Init_801230D0 to 87.04%.
smart_moves: Used Fighter_GObj* and GET_FIGHTER consistently for fighter-specific routines, avoiding generic HSD_GObj* typing in newly matched logic.
Modeled previously raw coordinate/collision data as ftCollisionBox, which clarified the data shape and supported the data split correction.
Introduced small static inline helpers for repeated callback setup and state cleanup, matching repeated compiler patterns while keeping the C readable.
Used PAD_STACK annotations in several functions where stack shape mattered, indicating a matching-focused approach rather than purely source-level cleanup.

Preserved Popo/Nana coupling explicitly through Player_GetEntityAtIndex, copied motion/velocity/ground state from Popo to Nana, synchronized facing direction with ftParts_80075AF0, and used accessory callbacks for partner position tracking.
Captured hitlag and damage/death callback behavior through efLib_PauseAll, efLib_ResumeAll, and ftNn_Init_80122FAC, which is important for the Ice Climbers linked-state behavior.
Kept known-mismatching or partial areas visible rather than overstating completion; the PR title itself flags remaining splitting, inline, and floatmath work.
decomp_lessons: For fighter decomp, replacing HSD_GObj* with Fighter_GObj* can be a useful type refinement when all uses are fighter-specific and GET_FIGHTER is central to the function.
When a data object is consumed by collision helpers such as ft_80082888 or ft_800824A0, check whether a typed collision-box struct matches better than raw Vec3 arrays.

Partner-character logic often requires exact mirroring of positions, velocities, facing direction, ground/air state, animation frame, and callbacks; these relationships can explain otherwise odd field copies.

Inline helper extraction can be necessary for matching repeated callback setup or state-reset sequences, but the PR still notes that inline work remains, so these helpers should be treated as evidence-backed tactics rather than final naming decisions.

Float math can remain a matching risk in functions using sinf, cosf, constants like M_PI_2, and comparisons such as 0.0 == attrs->x12C; future work should verify codegen rather than assuming source-equivalent math will match.
Split-file data boundaries may need to move after retyping data symbols; here ftNn_Init.c's data end moved by 4 bytes after collision data was reorganized.

Expanding attribute structs with named float offsets can unlock behavior decomp, but the names in this PR are still offset-based placeholders, so future passes should rename them only with stronger semantic evidence.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions remain address-based with the module prefix, e.g. ftNn_Init_801237F8 and ftNn_Init_80123954.
Move callbacks use existing Popo special naming such as ftPp_SpecialHi_0_Anim, ftPp_SpecialHi_4_Phys, ftPp_SpecialS_0_Coll, and ftPp_SpecialS_1_Anim even when implemented in Nana's init unit.
Temporary or not-yet-understood fields remain offset-style, e.g. x221F_b3, x2219_b5, x2071_b0_3, x1A5C, x2240, x2250, and ftIceClimberAttributes.x13C.

Static inline helpers are named by appending _inline or numbered inline variants to the function they support, e.g. ftNn_Init_801232A4_inline, ftNn_Init_80123B3C_inline, ftPp_SpecialS_0_Coll_inline2, and ftPp_SpecialS_1_Coll_inline3.
Unknown data symbols retain Unk-style names, e.g. ftNn_Unk_28 and ftNn_Unk2_803CDD60, even after receiving better structural types.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted PAD_STACK(8), PAD_STACK(0x10), and similar padding in functions where stack layout influenced matching.
Used explicit return; bodies for empty callbacks rather than empty braces in many IASA/Phys/Coll no-op callbacks, likely to preserve expected codegen.
Used static inline helpers to reproduce repeated callback/state setup patterns without forcing separate callable functions.
Used motion-state constants and flags directly, such as motion IDs 359, 360, 362, 365, 0x169, and flags like 0x0C4C508A or 0x0C4C528A, instead of inventing semantic enum names without evidence.
Matched float behavior with explicit 0.0f, 1.0f, sinf/cosf, M_PI_2 multiplication, and preserved unusual expression shapes such as negated position formulas.
Retyped raw data to ftCollisionBox and updated the split boundary, suggesting that data layout and symbol typing were part of the matching strategy.

The bot report confirms exact full matches for 24 functions and near-matches for several larger routines, showing the tactics were effective but not complete for ftPp_SpecialS_0_Coll, ftPp_SpecialS_1_Coll, ftNn_Init_80123954, and ftNn_Init_801230D0.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
Automated decomp.dev feedback reported GALE01 matched code at 43.30%, up 0.08% and 3168 bytes.
Automated feedback listed 24 newly matched functions in main/melee/ft/chara/ftNana/ftNn_Init.
Automated feedback listed 4 partial improvements, with ftPp_SpecialS_0_Coll at 99.13%, ftPp_SpecialS_1_Coll at 92.15%, ftNn_Init_80123954 at 74.59%, and ftNn_Init_801230D0 at 87.04%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish splitting ftNn_Init.c or move functions into more specific Nana/Popo special files.
Search for later renames of ftIceClimberAttributes fields xB0, xB4, xD0, x12C, x130, x134, x138, x13C, x140, x144, x148, and x14C.
Search for later matching fixes to ftPp_SpecialS_0_Coll, ftPp_SpecialS_1_Coll, ftNn_Init_80123954, and ftNn_Init_801230D0.
Check whether ftNn_Unk_28 and ftNn_Unk2_803CDD60 receive semantic names or are moved after data splitting.
Look for subsequent PRs resolving the inline/floatmath concerns mentioned in this PR title.
